backtest.title: Backtest
backtest.subtitle: Run your strategy against historical data and see how it performs.
backtest.clickToSelect: Click to select a CSV file
backtest.tickerDisabled: Add a data API key to enable search
backtest.barsCached: ⚡ {{count}} bars cached — no API call needed
backtest.fixedQtyDesc: Uses the quantity set in each strategy rule.
backtest.allInDesc: Buys with <1>cash × {{leverage}}x</1> at each entry signal. Re-entry is blocked while a position is held.
backtest.allInDescPlain: Buys with cash × {{leverage}}x at each entry signal. Re-entry is blocked while a position is held.
backtest.wholeUnitDesc: Quantities floored to whole units; orders < 1 unit are skipped.
backtest.errSetDates: Please set start and end dates.
backtest.errNoStrategy: No strategy selected. Build one first.

analytics.noRunsYet: No runs yet. Run a backtest to see results here.
analytics.selectRunTitle: Select a run to view analytics
analytics.selectRunDesc: Click any saved run in the sidebar to see its equity curve and performance metrics.
analytics.noAiModel: No AI model configured. Add a model key in Key Manager to use this feature.
analytics.askAiAnalyst: Ask the AI analyst about this run. Using <1>{{model}}</1> · temp {{temp}}
analytics.noEquityCurve: No equity curve data for this run.
analytics.confirmDeleteBatch: Delete {{count}} selected run(s)? This cannot be undone.
analytics.confirmDeleteAll: Delete all {{count}} saved run(s)? This cannot be undone.
analytics.totalReturnDesc: Total gain/loss from starting capital. (final − initial) / initial × 100.
analytics.cagrDesc: Compound Annual Growth Rate — annualised return if the run lasted one full year.
analytics.maxDrawdownDesc: Largest peak-to-trough equity drop while a position was held. More negative = worse.
analytics.sharpeRatioDesc: Return per unit of volatility (annualised). >1 is good, >2 is excellent. 0 if no volatility.
analytics.winRateDesc: % of closed round-trip trades that were profitable. High win-rate can still lose if losses are large.
analytics.profitFactorDesc: Gross profit ÷ gross loss. >1 means profits outweigh losses. ∞ means no losing trades.
analytics.sortinoRatioDesc: Like Sharpe but only penalises downside volatility. >1 is good, >2 is excellent.
analytics.calmarRatioDesc: CAGR ÷ |max drawdown|. Measures return earned per unit of drawdown risk. Higher is better.
analytics.totalTradesDesc: Total number of fill events (each buy/sell/short/cover counts as one).
analytics.avgTradeDesc: Mean P&L per completed round-trip as % of entry price.
analytics.avgBarsHeldDesc: Mean number of bars a position was held before being closed.

strategy.title: Strategy Builder
strategy.subtitle: Build trading rules for each role. Every entry needs a matching exit rule — warnings will appear if something is missing.
strategy.name: Name
strategy.noRulesYet: No rules yet — add one to get started
strategy.noRulesForRole: No rules for this role yet
strategy.couldNotLoad: Could not load strategy — config may be empty.
strategy.warnBuyNoSell: You have Buy (Long) rules but no Sell (Exit Long) rule. Positions will never close!
strategy.warnSellNoBuy: You have Exit Long rules but no Entry Long rule — nothing will open the trade.
strategy.warnShortNoExit: You have Enter Short rules but no Exit Short rule. Short positions will never close!
strategy.warnExitNoShort: You have Exit Short rules but no Enter Short rule — nothing will open the short.
strategy.warnNoConditions: Rule "{{name}}" has no conditions and will never fire.

indicator.title: Indicator Builder
indicator.subtitle: Drag building blocks onto your indicator canvas. Each indicator is a reusable signal you can reference in your strategies.
indicator.createNew: Create a new indicator to get started
indicator.dragBlocks: Drag blocks here to build your indicator
indicator.deleteUsedConfirm:
"{{name}}" is used in {{count}} strategy(s): {{strategies}}.

Delete it anyway?

analyzer.title: Analyzer
analyzer.selectToStart: Select a strategy or indicator to start
analyzer.pickToBegin: Pick a strategy or indicator from the left panel to begin.
analyzer.configureFirst: Configure an AI model in Key Manager first
analyzer.loadedStrategy:
Loaded strategy: **{{name}}**

Select a prompt below for an objective assessment, or type your own question.
analyzer.loadedIndicator:
Loaded indicator: **{{name}}**

Select a prompt below for an objective assessment, or type your own question.

strategyPrompts.conditions: What market conditions does it suit best?
strategyPrompts.overfitting: Does it have overfitting or look-ahead bias risks?
strategyPrompts.buyHold: How does it compare to a buy-and-hold approach?
strategyPrompts.missingRules: Are there missing exit rules or edge cases?
strategyPrompts.sensitivity: How sensitive is it to parameter changes?

indicatorPrompts.range: What is the output value range?
indicatorPrompts.signals: How should I interpret its signals?
indicatorPrompts.limitations: What are its limitations or caveats?
indicatorPrompts.lookback: What lookback period does it require?
indicatorPrompts.useInStrategy: How could I use this in a strategy?
indicatorPrompts.compare: How does it compare to similar standard indicators?

aiStrategy.title: AI Strategy Builder
aiStrategy.subtitle: Natural language → Rules
aiStrategy.welcome: Hi! Describe your trading strategy in plain English and I'll convert it to rules. For example: "Buy when a 20-day EMA crosses above a 50-day EMA. Sell when RSI exceeds 70."
aiStrategy.customIndicatorHint: You have {{count}} custom indicator(s): {{names}}. Reference them by name in your prompt. To use non-default parameter values, mention them explicitly — e.g. "use RSI Oversold with period 10 and threshold 25".

aiIndicator.title: AI Indicator Builder
aiIndicator.subtitle: Natural language → Expression trees
aiIndicator.welcome: Hi! Describe a technical indicator you want to create, and I'll generate the expression tree. For example: "RSI oversold signal that returns 1 when RSI(14) is below 30" or "Moving average distance measured as percentage"

keys.title: Key Manager
keys.subtitle: Store multiple API keys for data providers and AI models. Switch the active one anytime — only the active key is used.
keys.dataProvidersDesc: Store multiple data provider keys and switch the active one anytime. Only the active provider is used for API fetches.
keys.aiModelsDesc: Store keys for multiple AI providers and switch between them. Only the active model is used for AI features.
keys.noDataKeys: No data keys saved yet. Add one below.
keys.noAiKeys: No AI model keys saved yet. Add one below.
keys.label: Label
keys.pasteApiKeyAutoDetect: Paste API key here — provider auto-detected
keys.fetchedLive: Fetched live · or pick from the static list below
keys.providerMismatch: Provider mismatch: "{{model}}" requires a {{expected}} key, but the key looks like it's from {{detected}}. Please use the correct key.
